- People to talk to, preferably outside of X and even more preferably IRL. I already have a pretty good network of internet… https://t.co/16Eh2l13Xu

I've been thinking about this a ton the last week so I guess I'll post the X goals I want to achieve through gaining a non-zero amount of followers:

- People to talk to, preferably outside of X and even more preferably IRL. I already have a pretty good network of internet friends on my real account (and have met them IRL too), but I feel like tryharding twitter on a fresh account will break the mental block I have about continuing to meet more people?
- Career opportunities, I'm sick of my job and want to work at a place with cool people and not just random tech bro coworkers making a CRUD app. 
- Dopamine from Likes and Comments. Isn't that what everyone wants? I want to post something that I think is funny/interesting and have people say "I think that's funny/interesting too"
- A network of people to play with my little coding experiments. I have a lot of silly little things I've made over the years (discord bots, twitter bots, unity games) that are basically only used by me and 0-1 other people. It would be really cool to have some likeminded people to validate my fun projects and maybe kickstart some critical mass that's needed for more multi-person experiments and games

I finished Return of the Obra Dinn this week and I really liked it, I really appreciated the story telling style. Like, there's really no "story" other than like 2 plot points. It's more like just a painting of a specific point in time with a very specific vibe

And although I was really just trying to get the last ~third of the game over with quickly because I was already getting kinda frustrated I thought the style of investigation that the player is forced to do is really cool.

After finishing it I hopped on reddit and saw tons of people complaining "how was I supposed to know I needed to do that" but IMO the game did a really good job of hinting that you need to look at it with a certain perspective and not treat it like a regular game
Going back to work after playing this game for a whole day was really weird because I couldn't turn off the "investigate the mystery" part of my brain while solving code monkey JIRA tickets lmao
Add the Keurig to the list of products that everyone online says is garbage and useless but actually is really cool and brings me joy on a daily basis
Anyways, people always compare Obra Dinn with Outer Wilds and I think its a fair comparison. But Outer Wilds is like 8x better IMO

Other things on the list:

- Paid ad-free versions of things

Can I talk about my top album of all time, "Good Luck Everybody" by AJJ? https://t.co/zN8UHitSjM

Their earlier album, "The Bible II", was my GOAT before this. It was one of those pieces of art that felt like it was putting into words exactly how I felt about myself and my life and blah blah blah, and ended on this masterpiece about understanding and becoming your demons

And when Good Luck came out in 2020 I thought "great, another anti-trump message that I've heard 100 times from every other artist". And on first listen it was just that, really top tier music but overall it just felt like simple a "fuck trump, fuck the government" piece

and that's not BAD, some of AJJ's greatest songs are about "fuck government" and I'm all for that. But in 2020 I just wanted to tune out of that stuff after all these years. 

And then Covid happened a few months later and it recontextualized the whole thing for me.

This album to me feels like it was inexplicably written about covid (before it even existed) instead of the president. Instead of the overall message being "good luck with the elections, everybody", it became "oh god. good luck everybody, we're going to need it".
And inserted between these explicitly political songs are some really really personal things that didn't really click for me until lockdown and social isolation hit.

"Body Terror Song" wasn't just a slow filler song that I didn't understand. Suddenly I was empathizing with the lyrics about how our bodies are stupid and weak and ugly. The words "I'm so sorry that you have to have a body" really hit me hard back in those days.

"Maggie" almost makes me cry every time I hear it. A little beautiful song about a dog, completely detached from the political bullshit that was going on. For a lot of us during Covid, our dogs were the only thing holding us together and I fucking love it.

AJJ albums always seem to end with a massive banger with some really profound imagery in them, and "A Big Day for Grimley" didn't disappoint. After this whole album of pessimism and bleakness, we end on this slightly positive optimistic note that launches this album to #1 for me

Its another song about dealing with the changes that covid did to our minds and bodies. Like 2020 chewed us up and a lot of us turned into weird quiet fortresses of solitude, but theres always hope that the little things we used to take for granted might come back eventually?

A lot of this stuff doesn't feel as impactful these days when Covid and Trump are really not on everybody's mind, but in mid-2020 all this stuff I just wrote really made the album feel almost prophetic and easily the greatest album of all time to me.

Oh, also Sean hopped on live stream nearly daily for a whole year and just played covers in a series of "Live From Quarantine" shows that I highly suggest you watch. Dude is the greatest https://t.co/UdLGIqo0z2

Lore dump #threadapalooza #loreapalooza

1 - I grew up in a middle-ish class quiet suburb somewhere outside of los angeles and really didnt have an eventful childhood. I guess the most notable thing is that I met my best friend in like first or second grade, and he's still my best friend 30 years later

2 - My favorite book series was Animorphs and I'd day dream for hours on end about turning into a bird and just flying around and avoiding all the weird stuff that the daily life of a 8-13 year old contained

3 - My parents were mostly apolitical and non-religious, which I've grown to really appreciate over the years. I feel like I was never forced into some ways of religous or political thinking, and I think that made grow up being a lot more analytical and open minded about stuff

4 - in middle school i definitely had the feeling i was one of the "weird" kids. I remember spending almost all of my time digging up ant hills with a stick behind the "portables" and putting them in water bottles in some bad attempt at making ant farms

5 - I recently reconnected with the ant-hill friend this year and we've been playing baldurs gate and getting kbbq whenever I visit. Makes me so happy that I can still hang out with people from my childhood

6 - My dad plays music as a hobby and my parents told me I would like joining band class, so in 8th grade I started playing flute. I remember being first or second chair through all of middle school, which isnt really a high bar lol

7 - being a guy who plays the flute invited a lot of "haha gay" jokes, especially since we were like 14 years old and it was the mid '00s when EVERYTHING was "gay". whatever, I don't remember really caring about that

8 - in high school I was in marching band. the band teacher was basically right out of college and I think she was a little overwhelmed with teaching marching band? I really liked her but the band wasnt really skilled at all.

9 - The thing about high school marching band is that it amplifies the already built-in horniness of teenagers by putting them in a school bus for a 5 hour trip to competitions every month or so. The funny thing for me is that I was completely oblivious to all of it

10 - At one point this girl who played clarinet who I actually really liked asked me WHILE WE WERE BOTH ALONE IN A STORAGE CLOSET "if I asked you to kiss me right now, would you do it?" and my dumb ass just said "um idk lol" and completely missed the MASSIVE hint

11 - at some point my instincts finally kicked in and I was suddenly made aware of things like sexual attraction in like 9th grade. At that same point I think I actually started getting affected by the "haha gay flute player" jokes (they were actually just jokes and not mean)

12 - I dont remember what actually kicked it off but I had probably a full week where I had a kind of mental break down about my sexual orientation and came out of it with the realization that I'm bi.

13 - at the time (and still now) my point of view ended up being "why should someone's gender stop me from liking them or being in a relationship with them?" and I feel really comfortable with that being a core value to me these days

14 - also the distinction between bi and pan doesn't really exist to me, I just choose the "bi" label because thats just how i feel? idk, people have so many made up definitions of this stuff that it literally doesnt matter

15 - High school kinda destroyed me. I constantly felt like no one took me seriously because I was just a kid and they were adults, which i guess is a pretty universal experience. But it really messed me up on a deep level
16 - I spent more effort than I ever have on a project in history class once because I really liked the class and thought it was fun. The teacher gave me 99/100 because "nobody is perfect"

17 - I had always given P.E. class the bare minimum effort and had some horrible mile times. One time I actually decided to see how fast I could run the mile and was super proud of my time. Until the teacher told me that I must have cheated and she marked gave me 0

18 - I had an assignment where we needed to write 10 words and their definitions. The teacher gave me a 0/10 because I capitalized the first letter of the word, because "the dictionary writes them in all lowercase"

19 - on the other hand, I had a physics teacher who noticed I was doing really well on tests but not paying attention a lot in class and not doing the homework. We had a serious talk and he let me not do homework as long as I kept getting As on tests. best teacher ever tbh

20 - All this school bullshit taught me that nothing really matters i guess? Like what *really* was the purpose of 99% of school? I basically gave up because I honestly came to the conclusion that no matter how hard I try I'll still fail

21 -we had a "senior project" where we had to job shadow someone for like 6 months and write this massive report. The teachers constantly told us that if we didnt pass the class, we wouldnt get into college (lol). it ended up not mattering at all

22 - At the time I wasn't sure if they were lying about that fact, but I sure knew that I didnt really care about college so I didn't have a reason to do this project. I had so many friends and teachers basically begging me to not do something I'm going to regret and just do it

23 - anyways, back to early high school. After I came out to my friends as bi, I started dating another flute guy. I honestly can't remember much about our relationship which is really weird to me. Like I can't recall a single conversation with him
24 - This would actually end up being a trend in my life. I have a really bad memory for specific events, I only really can remember vibes from things that are more than like 2 years in the past.

25 - I still talk to him regularly these days and some of the story he tells about us and high school seem like completely made up stories that I don't remember, but I can't really dispute them lol. We broke up (twice?) some time in 10th grade

26 - I then started dating a girl who I've vaguely known since elementary school. I don't remember how I ended up in her social circle but we were hanging out at lunch and after school for a bit before we went out to a movie and I "asked her out"

27 - Our relationship was pretty good, we were really close even through a handful of implosions of our social circle. She went to college in Long Beach and I stayed home in North-ish LA and went to community college.

28 - "Its almost friday" was the theme of our whole little semi-long distance relationship because I would drive the hour down to the college every friday and hang out, then drive her back home to her parents house nearby for the weekend

29 - At some points, I had some severe feelings of anger? disdain? because she was having a hard time in college, socially. Almost every evening she would log on to AIM and I would just receive hours of ranting about stuff that I couldnt help with.

30 - This is something I struggle with a lot, and I'll write about that part of my psyche later in the thread, but a major theme in my life has been me thinking "what about my problems?" while helping my friends with all their issues

31 - We eventually moved in together with some roommates who ended up being massive jerks. Two bedrooms, and we split the rent by 1/3 between the three of us. The one guy's girlfriend moved in but we never ended up adjusting the rent split

32 - So my girlfriend and I were paying 66% of the rent despite being 50% of the people and being basically blocked from using the kitchen and living room, since the one roommate turned the living room into his PC gaming area

33 - whatever, it was cheap. GF and I moved into a studio about 200 feet from the beach in downtown Long Beach. I really miss that place despite how gross it (and the beach) was. We adopted a dog, we gave away her bird because the bird was too loud

34 - She worked at the university's cafeteria and I worked at a doggy day care, both making minimum wage part time. I got a job at a warehouse copy/pasting excel spreadsheet rows into a web store full time. The money was amazing for our  quality of life

35 - I taught myself Python while working at the spreadsheet factory in order to automate some work. My one coworker and I basically goofed off all day because we could do a week of work in 3-4 hours and our manager was cool with that.

36 - I left that place for a "real" coding job. A place in orange county hired me to make wordpress themes. at the time I thought "this is more money than I'll ever need, I can fix all of our problems now".
37 - We moved out of our 0 sqft $800 studio and into a 1 bed really nice apartment in costa mesa. We had a park nearby and a quiet neighborhood. There was a boba place across the street.

38 - My manager at this job ended up asking me to help him with his new Kickstarter for a niche subscription based clothing company he was starting. I said hell yeah that sounds sick, I've always wanted to start a company

39 - the kickstarter raised $60,000, we had 1000 customers paying $50/month, we had a pretty big warehouse full of clothes and I absolutely loved visiting it and just imagining this little operation getting huge (or getting bought)

40 - I owned 5% of the company for my work setting up the website and ordering flow, plus I had basically full freedom on the tech side of things. Looking back its kinda funny because I barely knew anything about coding and websites

41 - After some time of working on this in my free time after work, I started to get tired of it. We didn't really have a lot of growth, and I was getting left out the plans and trajectory of a lot of stuff so I decided to just tone down my efforts

42 - Every 6 months or so the owner would mention "oh yeah you're being diluted again. you have 1% now. you have 0.5% now". My dreams of getting rich if the company sold for $100 million turned into dreams of maybe getting a few thousand after years of coding for free.

43 - I told them I'm happy to help the code side of things in the future, but it would have to be for something that I find interesting to work on, not just boring e-commerce code. I havent talked to them since ~2016

44 - GF finished college and we decided to move to Colorado. We had some friends in the area and we really didn't have any reason to go anywhere else so we said YOLO and moved from california to colorado with our dog in 2018(?)

45 - I was able to work remotely at the job I already had, so I set up in the spare bedroom of our new apartment while she looked for work in the area. I was making enough money so I wasn't really stressed about this situation

46 - She found the job search extremely depressing. I think she went 6 months without a job, and had a handful of interviews. I kept telling her to find some hobbies or something to do outside but that didnt really work for her

47 - She had a really weird mental block where if something is a priority, it is the ONLY thing that gets worked on. No taking the dog out, no video games, absolutely nothing except stressing about the job search 24/7
48 - This was wearing down on both of us. She would come to bed and do the thing she always did and just trauma-dump her whole day to me, which consisted of the exact same stuff every day.

49 - I developed a strange sort of anxiety about finishing work and socializing with her at the end of the day. The daily dump was unavoidble and I would be a huge asshole if I just said "sorry, please dont talk about your problems", right?

50 - but at the same time, the only thing in my head is "what about my problems?". How do I interrupt someone talking about an extremely bad day in their life to be like "um sorry, its my turn"? Whatever, I learned to be good at just holding it in.

51 - If I have a great fun day at work and know she's feeling like this, I'm not supposed to just walk in and be like "guess what? I had so much fun today!". I think that era was the first time I thought about ending things

52 - things eventually got better, she got a bad job and then a good job. I got a new job at a local startup that actually had an interesting product and an amazing team of people. We were making tons of money.

53 - At this new job I like I was just hanging out with a bunch of friends all day instead of just grinding. It felt so amazing to go to the office and just build cool shit. I was one of 4ish engineers and had a big impact on the company as a whole

54 - A few of us played instruments and coordinated some jam sessions. We had basically a full four piece rock band and just played music all night. To this day its still the highest social experiences I've ever had

55 - something about being on a really well coordinated team at work and then taking that coordination and applying it to creating music was such an amazing magical thing. I've been chasing it ever since.

56 - There was a woman at work who randomly introduced herself and started talking to me about video games and stuff and we became really good friends pretty quickly. At some point the other engineer I worked with invited to lunch

57 - we started talking about girls (as dudes do) and he eventually asked me who I would be interested in at the office if I wasnt already in a relationship. I mentioned her and he told me that she's in an open relationship

58 - He not so subtly hinted that I should talk to her about that because she might also be interested in me. I'm not sure he realized just how much this idea sent my mind into some weird spiral in the next few months
59 - my surface level mental state is the sane reaction: "Oh, weird. Doesn't really matter to me though". But deep down I think I was going "you can just DO that??"

60 - GF and I were in a slowly declining dead bedroom and something like an open relationship SOUNDED like it could be the solution to our problem. Unfortunately (or not?) GF had already expressed that she would never be up for that, so I never brought it up.

61 - That one coworker and I had a really weirdly tense evening when I brought her to the bus stop to go home after work but the bus was late so we just sat in my car and talked about life in the dark for an hour
62 - God I wish she brought up her feelings about me so we could actually hash it out right then and there and put my mind to rest, but we didnt. I fucking hate complicated social situations, man.

63 - She eventually got a boyfriend and moved to california with him and we slowly drifted apart. In a recent conversation with her she did mention she *was* into me but didn't want to do/say anything because I already had a gf. Feels validated man

64 - After dating for 10 years and delaying because of Covid, the GF and I got married in late 2022. I have a weird aversion to applying labels to things so "girlfriend", "wife", "partner" weren't really distinct terms to apply to us

65 - And what I mean by that is getting married after all these years felt more like a "well we might as well do it" than a big life changing milestone to me. It was nice to see all my friends and family in one place so I had a great time.

66 - I mentioned that slowly declining dead bedroom earlier in the thread. Yeah that shit was at rock bottom around the time we got married. I'm embarrassed to say that every time we had sex I cried afterward because of how bad it felt

67 - I have a high-ish libido, and she had some body image issues that I could never quite get to the bottom of which blocked out all enjoyment of sex and intimacy for her. I was very clear that she never actually enjoyed sex and just did it for me

68 - and the thing about that is... it sucks. It feels like a trap. I desperately want sex (or even a hug lol) and once or twice a month she offers it to me. What am I supposed to do? I know I wont like it, and I know she wont like it.
69 - (nice) But what does it say if I refuse? I felt like it would destroy her self image. I desperately wanted her to feel desired and attractive and sexy but there was no easy path towards that.

70 - and look, the simple answer to this problem is "talk about it". We both had this mental block where "just talk about it" is this massive monumental wall that neither of us could get over. She brought up couples counseling and I very much agreed

71 - a few weeks and a christmas vacation later I brought up couples counseling again, and how we should get that started. She said she doesn't believe that anything would help us. I asked her how she could possibly even say that.

72 - I think that was the point I gave up entirely. After this mind crushing few months we finally decided to split up in January 2024. 12 years after we started dating and three months after our wedding.

73 - And the funny thing about that whole situation is that I was immediately doing great. It didn't feel like "the end" to me. To me, "the end" was the last 6 months of mental anguish and bullshit and being tired and angry and confused

74 - so telling friends and family and hearing "oh im sorry are you ok?" is always kind of funny because like... I'm free? I'm finally free and we should be celebrating this, because I've already mourned it months beforehand.

75 - I flew to my best friends house and we drank and talked about actual deep life stuff for the first time since our childhood. I took a road trip to my friends house from (63) and we had a great time hanging out and talking through some stuff

76 - I joined the local kink community and met some really kick ass people that fill me with joy every time I see them at the dungeon. I'm really just living life as a single guy to the fullest because I never had a chance to really BE single

77 - I'm not nervous or anxious about dumb things like constantly being worried about if my partner is enjoying my company or not. No more "well I want to do <x> but she wants to do <y> so I guess we're doing <y>" and its so amazingly freeing

78 - And for the first time in 12 years I get to really think about what my gender and sexuality mean to me? I couldn't bring myself to talk about this stuff to the ex for some dumb reason and never really got to explore it, so now I'm doing that.

79 - I visited an internet friend's house in seattle for a week and we messed around and laid in bed and goofed off all week in a weirdly sexual but platonic way and I definitely cried more than once that week

80 - because it had been so long since I've been able to just NOT worry about someone's feelings during a sexual encounter. And I don't mean that in a "the other person doesnt matter" way, i mean it like "its clear and unambiguous what they want"

81 - I've been slightly exploring gender expression, too. I painted my nails a handful of times before going to the kink club and it made me feel so good. and its funny because it made me feel STRONG and MASCULINE???

82 - Like fuck yeah I don't need to conform to society's dumb gender rules. I'm a man and I can do this shit because I want to. And I imagine thats not quite what most who are gender explore-y are feeling?

83 - and that stuff is the kind of stuff that I would be too *embarrassed* to do or think around my ex. Which is really strange because she was extremely open minded and would have immediately accepted anything I wanted to do.

84 - I've been talking to the guy I briefly dated in high school again and we're having some weird conversations. IDK, its probably not going to go anywhere but its kinda fun to have dumb flirty conversations with just another dude
85 - and this is the part where I notice i need to make 15 more of these and I'm running out of live events

86 - What are my goals for the future? This year I just really focused on learning to love myself and the solitude that the universe provided me and its been a wonderful experience. Next year I think I want to build up my friend circle again

87 - Covid and breakup really shattered my friend group and I only have one person who I could reliably see IRL. I'm going to try to get better at "putting myself out there" when I go out, maybe join some more social groups, sports(?), meetups

88 - and twitter has surprisingly been really good for this. I feel like if I really wanted to I could pivot some of these random online people who live in my phone to familiar friends who live in my phone

89 - and having severe FOMO about bay area stuff is really the thing I needed to motivate me to get a new job. My job pays me a ton, more than I ever could have imagined 5 years ago, but unfortunately I'd need more to feel comfortable moving to SF

90 - When I actually kick off my job search in 2024 I'll post some of my personal projects here. I have a couple of really interesting/funny/cool things that I've used AI for that I'd love to share but would reveal my IRL info so we'll see

91 - I actually feel comfortable with the idea of non-anonymizing this account because I really dont care anymore? When I made this account I wanted to break down that mental wall that stopped me from just TALKING because im afraid of being seen

My favorite new albums of 2023

Harley Poe - Little Joey and the Psychophallic Sex Brain
Harley Poe is the one artist I always try to get my friends to listen to, but they always say its too weird. I'm in love with the grotesque horror elements that have been present in almost every HP song and this album is no different. Joe is a little disgusting sex goblin (just like me) and his art is such a cool little window into a really strange mind

The Mountain Goats - Jenny from Thebes
I'm obsessed with TMG lore so I was extremely excited to learn that the new album was going to be about "Jenny", the recurring character that we've had little glimpses of for the past decade of Mountain Goats. Really sick album full of little nods to old TMG. IMO not as GOAT as Bleed Out, but still a top tier modern Mountain Goats album

AJJ - Disposable Everything
really loving how they brought back the fast jam style of songs for this album after a handful of really emotional albums. Amazing album to yell at the top of my lungs in the car with. Plus, my favorite "live only" song, "I wanna be your dog" finally got a studio version

Sufjan Stevens - Javelin
I really think Sufjan might be the greatest songwriter ever but after a handful of really weird albums I was kinda getting tired of new Sufjan. Javelin really feels like he took the best parts of the Adz and All Delighted People era and made a massive banger. I feel like this album takes a lot more energy to focus on than a lot of other styles of music but taking an hour to just vibe and feel it is always rewarding

if i was designing a game, i simply would ask "is this fun" for every game mechanic and not add things that arent fun

I'm looking at you: max carry weight,  inventory limits, stack size limits, sprint stamina outside of combat, fall damage, motion blur, actions that require you to hold a button for more than 5 seconds (eg mining some big rock)

and I think more gamers should think "is this fun?" as often as I do when they play a game instead of just mindlessly submitting to the aggressive addiction machine. 

When youre sitting there for 30 seconds mining a big rock, ask yourself "is this fun?" because its likely not

When your character is holding a bunch of loot and you need to spend 15 minutes walking to a store to dump it and then walking back to the mission area, is that fun? I really don't think so. It only exists to burn the player's time

Look I understand job searching is pretty taxing mentally but can we stop acting like applying to one job a day is this huge thing that takes up a large amount of time?

> Resume
You only need to make a resume once per job search. Maybe 1-2 alternates with specific vibes (I have one for startup jobs and one for corpo code-monkey jobs)

> Cover Letter
Arguably unnecessary but just write one with placeholders and copy paste it. No one is reading it anyway

> Application
I've never applied somewhere that takes more than 15 minutes, even with bs "answer in a paragraph" questions

> References
Literally just text your old coworkers and managers asking if you can use them as references. No one calls them anyway. You *are* networking and staying in contact with people who liked working with you, right?

> Linkedin
hmm nah

> Pointless indeed tests
Never seen one thats anything more than just the run of the mill application questions
